title: Tangy Tender French Celeriac Soup
description: A velvety, tangy soup with tender celeriac cubes, bright apple cider vinegar, and a splash of white wine, perfect for midnight cravings.

ingredients.name: coconut milk
ingredients.notes: nut‑free alternative for creaminess; omit for strict vegan

instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in diced carrots and celery, cooking another 2 minutes.
Add the celeriac cubes and bay leaves; cook for 5 minutes, letting the vegetables soften slightly.
Deglaze with white wine, scraping up any browned bits, and let the wine reduce by half, about 3 minutes.
Pour in vegetable stock, add apple cider vinegar, lemon juice, thyme, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cover and simmer until the celeriac is tender, about 30 minutes.
Remove the bay leaves and blend the soup until smooth using an immersion blender or regular blender in batches.
Return to the pot, stir in coconut milk, and warm through. Adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with a swirl of coconut milk and a sprinkle of fresh thyme if desired.
